recipes.title: Khachapuri
recipes.description: Georgian boat-shaped Adjarian cheese bread filled with molten sulguni and topped with a runny egg and butter.
steps: Bloom the yeast and sugar in the warm milk for 10 minutes until foamy.
Mix the flour, salt, egg, oil and milk mixture into a soft dough; knead for 10 minutes and rise covered for 1 hour until doubled.
Divide the dough into 4 pieces. Roll each into an oval, pile cheese along the centre and fold the long edges over to make a boat shape, pinching the ends.
Place the boats on a baking tray lined with parchment and rest for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 230°C and bake the khachapuri for 12 to 14 minutes until the dough is golden and the cheese is bubbling.
Pull each boat from the oven, make a well in the middle of the cheese, and slip an egg yolk into each.
Return to the oven for 1 to 2 minutes, just until the yolk is barely set, then add a knob of butter to each.
Eat by tearing pieces of the crust and dipping into the molten cheese, butter and yolk.

recipes.title: Khinkali
recipes.description: Georgian soup-filled dumplings with a twisted topknot, eaten with the fingers and seasoned with black pepper.
steps: Mix the flour, water and salt to a firm dough, knead for 10 minutes, wrap and rest for 30 minutes.
Combine the meats, onion, garlic, ice water, cilantro, coriander, caraway, salt and pepper into a slack, sticky filling.
Roll the dough thinly and cut into 12 cm rounds.
Place a heaped tablespoon of filling in the centre of each round.
Hold the disc in your palm and pleat the edge upward into many folds, twisting the top into a topknot to seal.
Boil the khinkali in well-salted water for 8 to 10 minutes after they float.
Lift out and serve with cracked black pepper. Eat by holding the topknot, biting a small hole, sipping the soup, then eating the rest; leave the topknot on the plate.

recipes.title: Chakhokhbili
recipes.description: Georgian chicken stew braised with tomatoes, onions and a generous amount of fresh herbs and spices.
steps: Brown the chicken pieces in oil in a wide pan; remove and set aside.
Add the butter and onions to the same pan and cook over medium heat for 20 minutes until very soft and golden.
Stir in the tomatoes and the spices and simmer for 10 minutes to a thick sauce.
Return the chicken to the pan, season, cover and braise gently for 35 to 40 minutes until very tender.
Stir in the crushed garlic and most of the herbs and rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle with the remaining herbs and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Lobio
recipes.description: Georgian stewed red kidney beans cooked with onions, herbs and spices, served with cornbread and pickles.

ingredients.name: dried red kidney beans, soaked overnight

steps: Drain the soaked beans and place in a pot with the water; bring to a boil and skim foam.
Simmer covered for 90 minutes until the beans are very tender.
Meanwhile, sauté the onion in oil for 15 minutes until soft and golden.
Crush the walnuts, garlic and spices into a paste using a mortar.
Stir the tomato paste into the onions, then add the walnut paste and warm through.
Tip the onion mixture into the beans, lightly mash about a third of the beans, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in the vinegar, salt and most of the herbs.
Serve hot in clay pots with the remaining herbs sprinkled on top.

recipes.title: Pkhali
recipes.description: Georgian cold appetizer of finely chopped vegetables bound with a walnut, garlic and herb paste, served as part of a supra.
steps: Wilt the spinach in boiling water for 30 seconds, plunge into ice water, drain and squeeze very dry, then chop finely.
Grate the cooked beetroot finely and squeeze out the juice.
Pound the walnuts, garlic, salt and spices in a food processor or mortar to a thick paste.
Add the vinegar and most of the herbs to the walnut paste and pulse to combine.
Divide the paste into two and stir one half into the spinach and the other half into the beetroot.
Adjust the seasoning of each, then shape into small mounds or quenelles on a platter.
Top with pomegranate seeds and the remaining herbs and serve cold or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Satsivi
recipes.description: Georgian holiday dish of poached chicken or turkey in a velvety walnut and garlic sauce, served cold.
steps: Simmer the chicken in salted water with a bay leaf for 35 minutes until cooked through; lift out, cool and reserve 600 ml of broth.
Sauté the chopped onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes until very soft, not browned.
Pound the walnuts, garlic, salt and all of the spices into a thick paste.
Stir the walnut paste into the cooked onions, then thin out with the reserved broth a little at a time to a creamy consistency.
Simmer the sauce gently for 10 minutes; do not boil hard or the walnut oil will separate.
Stir in the vinegar and adjust seasoning. Cool to room temperature.
Pull the chicken into large chunks, fold into the cooled sauce, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Sprinkle with cilantro and serve cold or at room temperature with bread.

recipes.title: Mtsvadi
recipes.description: Georgian skewered grilled meat marinated simply with onion and pepper, cooked over grapevine embers.

ingredients.name: dry red wine or pomegranate juice

steps: Toss the cubed pork with the sliced onions, salt and pepper, then pour over the wine.
Marinate covered in the refrigerator for 4 to 12 hours, mixing occasionally.
Light a charcoal grill, ideally with grapevine cuttings, and let it burn down to glowing embers.
Thread the pork tightly onto long metal skewers, leaving the onion behind.
Grill the skewers about 15 cm above the embers for 12 to 15 minutes, turning every 2 minutes, until charred outside and just cooked through.
Slide the meat onto a sheet of lavash to rest for 3 minutes.
Top with raw onion, pomegranate seeds and cilantro and serve with tkemali sauce.

recipes.title: Kharcho
recipes.description: Georgian rich beef soup thickened with rice, walnuts and tkemali sour plum, perfumed with lots of fresh herbs.
steps: Place the beef in a pot, cover with the water, bring to a boil and skim foam carefully.
Add bay leaves and 1 teaspoon of salt and simmer covered for 90 minutes.
Sauté the onions in oil until soft, then add the tomato paste and cook for 3 minutes.
Pound the walnuts, garlic and spices into a paste.
Add the rice, the onion mixture and the walnut paste to the pot and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the tkemali sauce and most of the herbs and rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Adjust seasoning, sprinkle with the remaining herbs, and serve with bread.

recipes.title: Badrijani Nigvzit
recipes.description: Georgian appetizer of pan-fried aubergine slices rolled around a garlicky walnut and pomegranate filling.
steps: Slice the aubergines lengthwise into 5 mm strips, salt them, and rest for 30 minutes; pat dry.
Heat the oil in a wide skillet and fry the aubergine strips for 2 to 3 minutes per side until deep golden and tender.
Drain on paper towels and let cool to room temperature.
Pound the walnuts, garlic, salt, pepper and all of the spices into a thick paste.
Loosen the paste with the vinegar and a splash of water until spreadable but not runny.
Fold in most of the chopped cilantro.
Spread a tablespoon of the walnut paste along each aubergine strip and roll up tightly.
Arrange seam-down on a platter, top with pomegranate seeds and the remaining cilantro, and serve at room temperature.
